summary: W26 was the first English-language cycle. No engagement data available yet (posts scheduled but not yet published at analysis time). W27 recommendations are built on research trends and best-practice patterns from English-speaking Threads communities. Priority: deploy 7 posts across the week with A/B hook testing (question-hook vs statement-hook) to begin building baseline data for future cycles.

hooks: Specific number hook: 'I made $X using 3 ChatGPT prompts' — credible and intriguing
Question hook: 'Are you still doing X the hard way?' — invites self-reflection
Bold statement hook: 'Stop trading time for money. There is a smarter way.' — scroll-stopper
Empathy hook: 'You are not lazy. You are just doing the wrong things.' — validates and reframes
Curiosity gap: 'The one thing top earners do differently (it is not what you think)' — compels reading
structures: Hook (1 bold line) → Problem framing → Solution list (3-5 items with emojis) → Soft CTA
Story arc: Past struggle → turning point → result → 'you can do this too'
List post: '5 ChatGPT prompts I use every morning' format — high save rate
Confession + lesson: 'The mistake that cost me clients' — high comment rate
themes: AI Tools That Make You Money (Priority 1 — highest engagement in English Threads market)
ChatGPT Prompts for Productivity (Priority 2 — high save rate, copy-paste value)
Morning Routine + Income Link (Priority 3 — evergreen, broad appeal)
Beginner Investing / Financial Freedom (Priority 4 — high save, finance affiliate potential)
Freelance Rate / Income Growth (Priority 5 — high comment rate from service providers)
Time Blocking / Deep Work (Priority 6 — broad professional audience)
Growing on Threads in 2026 (Priority 7 — meta content, community engagement)

try_next: A/B test: Question-hook variant vs Statement-hook variant for same theme (track which gets more comments)
All posts: open with specific number, reader pain point, or bold claim in line 1
End every post with a soft CTA: 'save this', 'try this today', 'which one will you start with?'
Use emojis as list markers (✅ → 💡 🔥) to improve visual scannability
Keep hashtags to 5-7: combine topic + action + audience tags (e.g. #AItools #sidehustle #productivity)
Post in English only — no Japanese. Tone: peer-to-peer, conversational, not guru-like
Keep posts under 200 words for Threads optimization — tight, punchy, no filler
avoid: Abstract or preachy tone ('It is important to...' 'You should always...')
Opening with 'Today I want to talk about...' or similar generic announcement
More than 7 hashtags — looks spammy to English-speaking audience
Content that could read as gatekeeping or elitist — keep tone accessible and empowering
Vague income claims without specifics — English audiences are skeptical of hype
priority_patterns: Priority Pattern A: Specific Number + List Format ('I made $X using these 5 methods') — drives saves and shares
Priority Pattern B: Story arc with concrete result ('A year ago I was broke, here is what changed') — drives comments and follows
Priority Pattern C: Empathy hook + actionable solution ('If you feel overwhelmed, try this') — drives saves and tags

approved_themes.theme: AI Tools That Actually Make You Money
5 ChatGPT Prompts That Save Hours Every Week
Morning Routine That Changed My Income
How to Double Your Freelance Rate
